behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from manifest files and included in vulnerability checks
behaviors.justification: Needs real file reading (package.json, requirements.txt, package-lock.json) to catch parsing bugs in nested dependency traversal. A mocked parser would hide failures where we skip transitive deps or mishandle nested structures.
behaviors.test_description: Read real manifest files (package.json with lock file, requirements.txt), extract all dependencies, verify every direct AND transitive dependency is in the final list by comparing against expected tree structure

behaviors.description: Vulnerability database query includes version ranges from manifest; results match those constraints, not just package names
behaviors.justification: Real failure: code queries API without version constraint (e.g., 'lodash' instead of 'lodash@^4.17.0'), receives CVEs for all versions, incorrectly reports vulnerabilities for versions not in use. A mocked API hides this—would always return exactly what we expect. Only real API behavior reveals if our assumptions are correct.
behaviors.test_description: Call real vulnerability database API (or highly realistic test double) with version-constrained queries, verify results contain only CVEs matching the manifest's version ranges, not unrelated versions of the same package

behaviors.description: CVE severity sorting places Critical > High > Medium > Low; CVEs of same severity sorted by CVSS score
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic business logic, no I/O or external calls. Sorting algorithm correctness can be fully tested in-process with fixture data.
behaviors.test_description: Create test CVE list with mixed severities and CVSS scores, verify output is sorted: all Critical before High, all High before Medium, etc.; within each severity, verify descending CVSS order

behaviors.description: Manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version; all other entries remain unchanged
behaviors.justification: Real failure: regex or string replacement corrupts manifest—e.g., updates 'lodash' and accidentally changes 'lodash-es' or mangles JSON. Requires real file reading/writing to catch these bugs; mocking file I/O hides them.
behaviors.test_description: Read real manifest file with multiple dependencies, modify one dependency's version, write back, re-read and verify: only that one dependency version changed, all other dependencies unchanged, manifest still valid/parseable

behaviors.description: Auto-generated pull request is created on real repository with correct single-dependency diff
behaviors.justification: Real failure: PR not created, wrong branch, wrong repo, or diff includes unrelated changes. Requires real Git operations and GitHub API calls. Mocked APIs cannot verify actual PR state on the real repository.
behaviors.test_description: Create PR on real test repository using real GitHub API, verify PR exists, verify the diff contains exactly one dependency version change (no other file modifications), verify branch, base, and target are correct

behaviors.description: Major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change using semver comparison
behaviors.justification: Pure semantic versioning comparison logic. Correctness is deterministic and testable in isolation—e.g., 1.0.0 → 2.0.0 is breaking, 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 is not.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparison: verify 1.0.0 → 2.0.0 (major bump) is flagged breaking, 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 (minor) is not, 1.0.0 → 1.0.1 (patch) is not; test edge cases (pre-releases, rc versions)

behaviors.description: When breaking change detected, PR creation is skipped and review flag is surfaced in scanner output
behaviors.justification: Real failure: major version bump detected but PR is still created on the real repo (breaking change not respected). Need real GitHub API call to verify PR was NOT created when it should have been skipped. Integration mocks could hide this if the mock is called but the flag is missing.
behaviors.test_description: Detect major version bump in manifest, verify: (1) no PR is created on the real repository, (2) scanner output contains a review flag indicating manual review needed, (3) the flag message is clear that a breaking change was detected
